title: China crude imports wane amid high stock levels
section_path: China crude imports wane amid high stock levels
section_path_text: China crude imports wane amid high stock levels
summary:
Main
China's crude imports registered a massive m-o-m decline in July, as top refiners were sensitive to recent price hikes on the back of record stockpiles. By Emma Li
China's seaborne crude imports slumped below 10mbd in July, down 13% m-o-m from June's 3-year highs. But imports have been higher compared to the same period in the past two years, as fuel demand has recovered from pandemic restrictions.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
China's crude imports registered a massive m-o-m decline in July, as top refiners were sensitive to recent price hikes on the back of record stockpiles. By Emma Li
China's seaborne crude imports slumped below 10mbd in July, down 13% m-o-m from June's 3-year highs.

children.title: New product export quotas will likely spur diesel exports
section_path: New product export quotas will likely spur diesel exports
children.section_path_text: New product export quotas will likely spur diesel exports
children.summary: Chinese refiners have increased diesel exports in July amidst improving export margins and cooling domestic demand, as summer harvest and planting come to an end and high temperatures and frequent rainfall dent infrastructure construction activities. But refiners are waiting for a new batch of product export quotas in August before ramping up exports further.

children.title: China imports finished gasoline as consumption tax on blending components supports short term arbitrage
section_path: China imports finished gasoline as consumption tax on blending components supports short term arbitrage
children.section_path_text: China imports finished gasoline as consumption tax on blending components supports short term arbitrage
children.summary: While state-run refiners continue exporting gasoline along with other clean fuels to utilise existing export quotas, they also purchased a few finished gasoline cargos in July for August arrival, as arbitrage economics flipped into positive territory in recent weeks, following the start of a new tax regime on blending components from end June, which dampened blending margins for private blenders and buoyed domestic gasoline margins for state-run refiners. But still, the recent imports are rare and likely short-lived.
